name: SYRIAN AMERICAN MEDICAL SOCIETY FOUNDATION
mission: SAMS works to serve the medical needs of patients in need, regardless of religious background, race, ethnicity, or political affiliation.
theoryOfChange: SAMS states it saves lives and alleviates suffering by delivering dignified, essential healthcare during crises, revitalizing health systems, and promoting medical education through a network of humanitarians; it also describes empowering local healthcare professionals through training and capacity-building to foster resilient health systems.

scoreSummary: SYRIAN AMERICAN MEDICAL SOCIETY FOUNDATION shows exceptional alignment and strong impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Hakam Sibai Award for Service (Syrian American Council)

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.sams-usa.net/zakat (Source: https://www.sams-usa.net/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 73% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Strong humanitarian service (+4)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 43/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, global health cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; high program ratio (≥85%) suggests efficient delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

judge_issues.message: The risk summary mentions a high domestic burn rate (73% of expenses stay in the US) but does not explain how this impacts the overall score. This is a key concern that should be addressed in the rationale.

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: High domestic burn: 73% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

risks.description: High domestic burn: 73% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

score_details.score_summary: SYRIAN AMERICAN MEDICAL SOCIETY FOUNDATION shows exceptional alignment and strong impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.sams-usa.net/zakat (Source: https://www.sams-usa.net/zakat)

all_citations.claim: SAMS maintains high financial accountability and efficient fundraising metrics.
The organization is zakat-eligible and maintains a specific zakat policy for donors.
The foundation reported total revenue of $61,395,982 and a program expense ratio of 95.6%.
SAMS provides extensive medical services inside Syria and specialized mental health support.
all_citations.source_name: Human Devastation Syndrome: The Impact of Conflict on Mental Health

all_citations.claim: The organization facilitates support groups for children in Jordan and Syria.
Therapeutic interventions by SAMS have led to a reduction in PTSD symptoms among patients.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: SAMS earns a high overall score due to its exceptional financial efficiency, direct service impact in high-urgency conflict zones, and clear alignment with donor values through zakat-eligible programming.
areas_for_improvement: Increasing working capital reserves beyond the current 4.6 months to enhance long-term sustainability
Expanding public reporting on specific cost-per-beneficiary metrics across all specialty programs

dimension_explanations.alignment: SAMS shows strong alignment with humanitarian and faith-based donor priorities as a ZAKAT-ELIGIBLE organization with a transparent zakat policy [2]. The foundation addresses high-urgency global health needs while maintaining a lean operational structure, spending only $0.02 to raise every dollar [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high impact through direct service delivery, maintaining a 95.6% program expense ratio and providing critical medical services inside Syria and neighboring countries [3][4]. Their specialized mental health interventions have shown documented success in supporting children and reducing trauma symptoms in conflict-affected populations [6][7].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Syrian American Medical Society Foundation provides life-saving medical relief and specialized healthcare to vulnerable populations regardless of background or affiliation.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 95.6%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach those in need
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.02 per $1 raised
Comprehensive Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS) programs that demonstrate measurable reductions in PTSD symptoms
baseline_narrative.summary: The Syrian American Medical Society (SAMS) Foundation is a humanitarian organization dedicated to delivering essential primary and specialty healthcare services to patients in crisis zones [1][4]. In the most recent fiscal year, the organization reported a total revenue of $61,395,982 to support its extensive network of medical missions and health centers [3].

all_citations.claim: Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid

all_citations.quote: Reduction in PTSD symptoms after therapy

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: SAMS earns a high overall score due to its exceptional financial efficiency, direct service impact in high-urgency conflict zones, and clear alignment with donor values through zakat-eligible programming.

areas_for_improvement.context: While program ratios are high, the charity could <cite id="1">publish consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for more granular cost-effectiveness analysis.
A significant portion of expenses, approximately <cite id="3">73% of total spending</cite>, is recorded as staying within the US despite the international nature of the mission.

bbb_assessment.summary: Maintains high governance standards with 100% independent board and audited financials.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization maintains a high cash-adjusted program expense ratio of 95.6% when accounting for specific grant cycles.
risk_factors: <cite id="3">95.6% of expenses stay in the US</cite> despite international mission focus
case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="3">high domestic burn rate</cite> where a large portion of expenses are recorded in the US, and a <cite id="3">95.6% program ratio</cite> that trails some international peers.

confidence.notes: Strong financial and impact data available despite lack of full CN rating.

known_gaps: Not fully rated by Charity Navigator

alignment.explanation: SAMS is an excellent fit for Muslim donors, offering <cite id="2">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and serving <cite id="2">asnaf categories including the poor and needy</cite> in Muslim-majority regions.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be improved by <cite id="1">expanding services to additional underserved geographies</cite> with limited existing nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: SAMS is highly credible due to its <cite id="1">28-year track record</cite> and its <cite id="1">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>. The organization maintains <cite id="1">100% independent board members</cite> and undergoes regular <cite id="1">financial audits</cite>.
credibility.improvement: Credibility could be further enhanced by <cite id="1">completing a full Charity Navigator rating</cite> to provide third-party verification of all financial beacons.

impact.explanation: The impact is driven by <cite id="4">direct medical service delivery</cite> and a <cite id="1">resilient 4.6 months of working capital</cite>. They have a <cite id="7">documented theory of change</cite> focused on specialized healthcare.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="1">publishing standardized cost-per-beneficiary metrics</cite> across their diverse program areas.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade B - Documented clinical outcomes and PTSD tracking

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Distributes medical supplies and funding to local health chapters and clinics

rich_narrative.headline: The Syrian American Medical Society Foundation provides life-saving medical relief and specialized healthcare to vulnerable populations regardless of background or affiliation.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support high-impact medical interventions in conflict zones through a zakat-eligible, faith-aligned institution.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat obligations through medical relief
Prioritize organizations with high fundraising efficiency
Value clinical evidence and mental health outcomes
Focus on the humanitarian crisis in Syria and Gaza
giving_considerations: Large-scale operations mean funds support a broad network of hospitals
High domestic expense reporting requires understanding of US-based support for foreign missions
Focus is on direct service rather than long-term advocacy
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking small-scale, local US community projects or those who prefer advocacy-focused organizations.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects verified outcome tracking for <cite id="7">PTSD symptom reduction</cite> and massive service volume data.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: SAMS provides specialized medical training and direct healthcare to rebuild human capital and alleviate suffering in crisis-affected regions.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Clinical evidence ensures that medical interventions are actually improving patient health outcomes in high-stress environments.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="1">54.2% revenue CAGR</cite>, SAMS is rapidly expanding to meet the growing medical needs in Gaza and Ukraine.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: 10 countries including Syria, Gaza, and Ukraine

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="1">$0.01 fundraising efficiency</cite> compared to a <cite id="1">peer program ratio median of 88.2%</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Specific focus on pediatric medical relief
similar_organizations.name: Palestine Childrens Relief Fund

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focus on global health and nutrition
similar_organizations.name: Helen Keller International

strengths.detail: SAMS operates with a highly lean model, spending only <cite id="1">$0.02 per $1 raised</cite>, which is significantly better than industry benchmarks.
The organization tracks specific clinical outcomes, including a <cite id="7">reduction in PTSD symptoms</cite> through its specialized MHPSS programs.
The foundation has successfully delivered over <cite id="4">3.5 million medical services</cite> to patients in need regardless of background.

strengths_deep_dive: SAMS Foundation's operational strength lies in its ability to maintain high-intensity medical services in active conflict zones. By managing <cite id="3">$18.2 million in foreign grants</cite> to local implementing partners, they ensure that aid reaches the most hard-to-access areas. Their governance is supported by an <cite id="1">independent board of 8 members</cite>, and they have achieved the <cite id="1">Candid Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite>, the highest level of disclosure available for nonprofits.
rich_narrative.summary: The Syrian American Medical Society (SAMS) Foundation is a premier humanitarian organization that has provided <cite id="4">3.5 million medical services</cite> to vulnerable populations since its inception. Founded in <cite id="1">1998</cite>, the organization specializes in delivering <cite id="4">life-saving medical relief</cite> in crisis zones, with a primary focus on the Syrian conflict where it has facilitated <cite id="4">3.2 million services inside Syria</cite> alone. The foundation operates a sophisticated network of <cite id="4">medical missions and health centers</cite> across Syria, Jordan, Turkey, and Gaza, addressing critical gaps in healthcare infrastructure. Financially, SAMS demonstrates significant scale, reporting <cite id="3">$99,929,862 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year. The organization maintains a <cite id="3">95.6% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring that the majority of its resources are directed toward direct medical care and psychosocial support. Beyond emergency surgery and primary care, SAMS is a leader in <cite id="7">Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS)</cite>, implementing programs that have shown a <cite id="7">measurable reduction in PTSD symptoms</cite> among conflict-affected survivors. Their operations are characterized by <cite id="1">exceptional fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending only <cite id="1">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in donations. This lean administrative model allows for a <cite id="1">working capital ratio of 4.6 months</cite>, providing a resilient financial cushion for rapid response in volatile environments.
